lens: Risk-on snapback: yesterday's Iran-driven selloff reversed as a record memory-chip rally lifted the Nasdaq 100 ~1% and crude gave back ~1.2%. Advisory-levered stocks led the rebound — Evercore +4.7%, Lazard +4.3%, Jefferies +4.2%, Goldman +3.0% — and the deal tape stayed busy: RBC and JPMorgan are bookrunning MDA Space's US$819M raise for its CLS buy, while Jefferies and Morgan Stanley defend Genco against a hostile tender.

stories.headline: Risk-on snapback: a record memory-chip rally lifts the Nasdaq 100 ~1% as yesterday's Iran selloff reverses
stories.why_it_matters: Wednesday's risk-off broke Thursday. A record in monthly memory sales (~$74.6B) drove a semiconductor rally — Micron, AMD, and Western Digital each up ~7-8% — pushing the Nasdaq 100 up ~1.05% even as mega-cap AI names (Nvidia, Meta, Alphabet ~-2%) lagged. WTI crude gave back ~1.2% to $72.64 as the oil market shrugged off the US-Iran escalation, and the VIX eased ~1% to 16.72. A calmer, risk-on tape is the friendlier backdrop for launching and closing processes.
stories.suggested_action: Use the tone reversal as a reason to re-engage clients who paused on Wednesday's volatility — a steadier tape is when stalled processes get relaunched, and that's the moment to lock in room infrastructure.

deal_clock.event: Nasdaq 100 +1.05% on record memory-chip sales; VIX -1.07% to 16.72
WTI -1.2% to $72.64 as oil shrugs off Iran escalation

stories.headline: Rates little changed — 10Y eases to ~4.56% the day after hawkish June FOMC minutes
stories.why_it_matters: A day after the hawkish June minutes (the first under Chair Warsh), Treasury yields drifted slightly lower, with the 10Y down under a basis point to ~4.561%. The move is small — financing conditions remain the central constraint on leveraged deal math, and there's no signal here that a near-term cut is back on the table. The overhang from Wednesday's hawkish read stays largely intact.
stories.suggested_action: No change to the leveraged-finance message: processes penciling on a near-term cut should still be stress-tested at current rates. Use the steadier tape, not lower rates, as the reason to re-engage.

deal_clock.event: 10Y yield eases to ~4.561% (-0.8bps)

stories.headline: Advisory-levered stocks lead the rebound — Evercore +4.7%, Lazard +4.3%, Jefferies +4.2%
stories.why_it_matters: The same pure-play advisory names that fell hardest Wednesday led Thursday's rebound: Evercore +4.69%, Lazard +4.34%, and Jefferies +4.17%, versus GS +2.95%, MS +2.50%, and JPM +1.23%. Rate- and sentiment-sensitive M&A franchises are trading on the deal-fee outlook, and the sharp two-way moves this week underscore how tightly the market is watching advisory momentum heading into July 14-15 bank earnings.
stories.suggested_action: Context for coverage calls: advisory-franchise stocks are moving on deal-flow expectations, so bankers are focused on execution and win-rate right now — a natural opening for a speed-to-launch, template-based room pitch ahead of earnings.

deal_clock.event: GS/JPM (Jul 14) and MS (Jul 15) Q2 earnings

stories.headline: RBC and BMO lead MDA Space's upsized US$819M bought deal to fund its ~€567M CLS acquisition
stories.why_it_matters: MDA Space upsized its bought deal to US$819M (23M shares at US$35.60), with RBC Capital Markets and BMO as joint lead bookrunners and JPMorgan, BofA Securities, and Scotiabank as joint active bookrunners. Proceeds fund MDA's ~€567M / US$648M purchase of ~70% of France's CLS from CNES. A live, multi-bank ECM execution funding a cross-border acquisition — exactly the fast-turnaround, syndicate-wide diligence workflow where clean access control matters.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to RBC's ECM/space-and-defense coverage on the MDA raise — a syndicated bought deal funding a cross-border acquisition is a strong, current reason to talk diligence infrastructure across the bookrunner group.

deal_clock.event: MDA announces ~€567M CLS acquisition from CNES
Bought deal upsized to US$819M; RBC & BMO joint lead bookrunners

stories.headline: JPMorgan is a joint active bookrunner on MDA Space's US$819M offering
stories.why_it_matters: JPMorgan is a joint active bookrunner alongside BofA Securities and Scotiabank on MDA Space's upsized US$819M raise (July 9), which funds MDA's ~€567M acquisition of ~70% of CLS. A live ECM syndicate role tied to a cross-border space/data deal — a concrete, current touchpoint for JPMorgan's TMT/space coverage even as its bulge-bracket mandates rarely surface in daily press.
stories.suggested_action: Connect with JPMorgan's ECM/space coverage on the MDA syndicate — a live bookrun funding a cross-border acquisition is a clean hook for a diligence-access conversation.

deal_clock.event: JPMorgan named joint active bookrunner on MDA's US$819M deal

stories.headline: BofA Securities joins the MDA Space US$819M bookrunner group
stories.why_it_matters: BofA Securities is a joint active bookrunner on MDA Space's upsized US$819M offering (July 9), which finances the ~€567M CLS acquisition. High simultaneous deal flow is BofA's profile, and a live cross-border ECM syndicate role is a rare, dated, public touchpoint to anchor an operational-efficiency conversation.
stories.suggested_action: Pitch syndicate-wide access efficiency to BofA's ECM coverage on the MDA raise — at their volume of concurrent mandates, repeatable room setup is a direct cost lever.

deal_clock.event: BofA Securities named joint active bookrunner on MDA's US$819M deal

stories.headline: Jefferies advises Genco as its board rejects Diana Shipping's $24.80/share hostile tender
stories.why_it_matters: Genco Shipping & Trading's board unanimously rejected Diana Shipping's $24.80/share cash tender offer, with Jefferies acting as financial adviser and Morgan Stanley as special adviser to the board (statement July 8; tender deadline July 10). A live hostile-tender defense is a high-intensity, document-heavy process — the kind of contested M&A situation where a controlled, auditable data room is central to the defense.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to Jefferies' shipping/defense-advisory team on the Genco situation — a live hostile tender on a tight clock is an immediate, concrete reason to talk secure diligence and board-materials management.

deal_clock.event: Genco board unanimously rejects Diana's $24.80/share tender; Jefferies advising
Diana's tender offer deadline

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley is special adviser to Genco's board on the Diana Shipping tender defense
stories.why_it_matters: Morgan Stanley is special adviser to Genco Shipping & Trading's board alongside Jefferies as its unanimously rejects Diana Shipping's hostile $24.80/share tender (July 8; deadline July 10). A live, contested tender defense is one of the most access-sensitive M&A processes — a fresh, deal-specific reason to engage MS's coverage team.
stories.suggested_action: Connect with Morgan Stanley on the Genco defense — contested tenders concentrate the most sensitive board and diligence materials, exactly where permissioned access is most defensible.

deal_clock.event: Morgan Stanley named special adviser to Genco's board
Diana's tender offer deadline

stories.headline: Guggenheim advises Destination XL as its board rejects Zodiac Partners II's revised $0.84/share tender
stories.why_it_matters: Destination XL Group's board reiterated its unanimous recommendation that stockholders reject Zodiac Partners II's revised, unsolicited $0.84/share cash tender as undervalued, with Guggenheim Securities as financial adviser (reiterated July 9; offer expires July 24). A live retail take-under defense is a document- and timeline-driven process — a current, deal-specific reason to engage an under-covered elite boutique.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to Guggenheim on the Destination XL defense — an active, contested tender with a July 24 clock is a concrete hook for a secure-diligence and board-materials conversation.

deal_clock.event: DXL board reiterates rejection of Zodiac's revised $0.84/share tender; Guggenheim advising
Zodiac's revised tender offer expires

stories.headline: PJT Partners retained to run the sale of a former Tether CIO's stake in the stablecoin giant
stories.why_it_matters: PJT Partners has been retained to run the sale of a portion of former Tether CIO Richard Heathcote's ~1.26% stake in Tether Holdings, per Bloomberg reporting (July 6). A private secondary in a company valued in the hundreds of billions means a tightly controlled, confidential diligence process for a small buyer universe — a natural private-markets data room use case for a covered boutique.
stories.suggested_action: Engage PJT's private-capital/secondaries coverage on the Tether stake process — a confidential, high-value secondary is exactly where controlled access and audit trails are the pitch.

deal_clock.event: Bloomberg reports PJT retained to sell part of a former Tether CIO's stake

stories.headline: Moelis retained to review Ticketek-owner TEG's ~$1B debt and raise fresh capital
stories.why_it_matters: Silver Lake-owned TEG (owner of Ticketek) has retained Moelis to review its ~$1B debt structure and raise 'hundreds of millions' in fresh capital, per the Australian Financial Review (~July 8). A live capital-structure/restructuring mandate involves multiple creditor and investor classes with differentiated access — a core Intralinks restructuring use case for a covered boutique.
stories.suggested_action: Connect with Moelis' restructuring/capital-advisory team on the TEG situation — multi-creditor capital raises need granular, litigation-grade access controls across classes.

deal_clock.event: AFR reports Moelis retained to review TEG's ~$1B debt and raise fresh capital

A live syndicated ECM execution tied to an acquisition is a strong, current data room use case across the bookrunner group.

pipeline_watch.note: Prospective / rumored situations — verified at publisher + date level; treat as Speculative and confirm before quoting on a call. 'Covered-bank leads' name one of your banks; 'situations to watch' are real but the mandate is unnamed or held by a non-covered adviser. (No covered bank was cleanly named as adviser on this cycle's rumors — all are advisers-not-disclosed.)

situations_to_watch.situation: Board 'minded to accept' an improved ~£5.2B / ~$7.3B take-private from US investor Castlelake; break-up speculation; adviser not disclosed
Received a buyout approach from Vista Equity Partners and Quinti Capital ('sources say'); adviser not disclosed
Weighing a take-private amid buyout-firm interest; reported to have held investment talks with Apollo; adviser not disclosed
Exploring a sale of its STAR debit-card network, reportedly in talks with large US banks; adviser not disclosed
Activist Jana Partners built a ~10% stake and is publicly pushing the cloud banking-software firm toward a sale; adviser not disclosed
Activist Ananym Capital urging the life-sciences tools firm to consider selling itself; adviser not disclosed
Activist Starboard Value reportedly building a stake; shares jumped after-hours; adviser not disclosed

skeptics_corner.note: Unverified leads — sourced from subscription tools (PitchBook / Mergermarket) or single-source reports that we could NOT confirm against a public source. Shown transparently so reps can chase them, not quote them. Verify before using on a call.

sector_heat.note: Which sectors are driving M&A right now — lead outreach with bankers in the hottest verticals. Carried from H1 2026 league-table and mid-year outlook coverage; today's live mandates skew to space/defense ECM and contested public-company tenders.

ranked.read: In focus today — a record ~$74.6B monthly memory-sales print drove a 7-8% chip rally (Micron, AMD, Western Digital) and led the tape; tech/AI remains the 2026 M&A volume leader despite mega-cap wobble.
ranked.source_note: Investing.com / PwC TMT mid-year outlook, Jul 2026

ranked.read: Unusually active — two live hostile/unsolicited tenders today (Diana/Genco with Jefferies + Morgan Stanley; Zodiac/Destination XL with Guggenheim), the most access-sensitive processes there are.
MDA Space's upsized US$819M raise (RBC, BMO, JPMorgan, BofA, Scotiabank) funds a cross-border CLS acquisition — capital-markets execution funding strategic M&A.
Consolidation wave still breaking — the Fiserv STAR debit-network sale talks with big US banks are live this week; activist pressure building on fintech (Alkami).

talking_point.quote: This week you saw both sides of it — advisory stocks got hit hardest Wednesday on the risk-off, then led the rebound Thursday, up 4-5% at the boutiques. The market is trading your franchises purely on deal-flow expectations. With Q2 earnings next week, how are you framing the pipeline — is the tape steady enough now to relaunch the processes that stalled in June?
talking_point.use_with: M&A and advisory coverage teams (esp. at Evercore, Lazard, Jefferies, Goldman, Morgan Stanley)
talking_point.context: The two-way move is the freshest, most concrete hook in today's brief: EVR/LAZ/JEF fell hardest on Wednesday's Iran-driven risk-off, then rallied 4-5% on Thursday's snapback, versus +1-3% for the bulge bracket. It sets up a natural pre-earnings (GS/JPM Jul 14, MS Jul 15) conversation about pipeline confidence and relaunching stalled processes — the moment room infrastructure gets revisited. Ties directly to today's live mandates (RBC/JPM on MDA, Jefferies/MS on Genco).

market_snapshot.macro_note: A risk-on snapback reversed Wednesday's Iran-driven selloff: a record ~$74.6B monthly memory-sales print drove a semiconductor rally (Micron/AMD/Western Digital +7-8%), lifting the Nasdaq 100 ~1.05% even as mega-cap AI names (Nvidia/Meta/Alphabet ~-2%) lagged. WTI crude fell ~1.2% to $72.64 as oil shrugged off the US-Iran escalation, and the VIX eased ~1% to 16.72. Rates were little changed a day after the hawkish June FOMC minutes, with the 10Y at ~4.561% (-0.8bps). Advisory-levered financials led (EVR +4.7%, LAZ +4.3%, JEF +4.2%; GS +3.0%). (Levels are intraday ~10:25 a.m. ET Jul 9, not official closes; 2Y Treasury omitted — feed unverified.)
